sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 8.78 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.8)

statement.latex: It is known that there exists a countable locally finite group that contains a copy of every other countable locally finite group. For which other classes of countable groups does a similar ``largest'' group exist? In particular, what about periodic locally soluble groups? periodic locally nilpotent groups?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
